initial_request_score.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: The aim of the current study was to assess neurobehavioural changes in a mouse model of extinguished chronic colitis and to explore associated changes along the gut-brain axis.
Repeated administration of 2% dextran sulphate sodium (DSS) was used to induce chronic colitis in C57BL/6 mice
confirmed upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9

issues.claim: Reduced spontaneous activity and upregulation of inflammatory gene expression
issues.reason: The text states the aim was to 'assess' changes, not specifically to find 'reduced' activity as a pre-defined goal; this is a result framed as a goal.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Change quantifiable_goal to 'Assess changes in spontaneous activity and inflammatory gene expression' to match the study aim.

evidence.quote: Repeated administration of 2% dextran sulphate sodium (DSS) was used to induce chronic colitis in C57BL/6 mice, followed by a recovery period of 3 weeks
median disease activity index (DAI) of 0 IQR [0-0], cDSS: median DAI of 0.5 [0.5-0.88], P<0.0001
Behavioural testing was performed at baseline and after the recovery period and compared with control mice.
cDSS mice exhibited significantly reduced spontaneous activity compared with their baseline behaviour P=0.0084 and with the controls P=0.0186
RNA sequencing was performed of the distal colon and choroid plexus
WGCNA ... revealed a consensus module of 123 co-expressed genes that differed significantly between the control and cDSS mice (P=0.00001721 )
qPCR on different brain regions (frontal cortex, hippocampus and choroid plexus) confirmed upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9
Acute colitis was associated with activation of microglia in the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, persisting in the prefrontal cortex of mice that recovered from chronic colitis
Behavioural testing was performed... qPCR was used... microglia... assessed by Iba1 immunofluorescence
P=0.0186... upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9... P=0.00001721
Conclusion: In this mouse model of chronic extinguished colitis reduced spontaneous activity was seen... Moreover, mice that recovered from colitis exhibited persisting neuroinflammation.
